Quantization is the process of reducing the precision or scale of a machine learning model's output to make it more efficient and faster to train on. In the context of language models like those used by GPT-3, quantization involves converting the raw numerical values produced by the model into a smaller range of numbers that can be processed by the hardware.

For example, if a language model produces an output value of 1024, it might be converted to a value between 0 and 255 (the maximum possible integer value). This conversion reduces the size of the output data, which can improve performance during training and inference
